title: Gale Duality, Blowups and Moduli Spaces
speaker: Carolina Araujo
abstract: To understand the birational geometry of a projective variety $X$, one seeks to describe all rational contractions from $X$. From an algebraic perspective, information about all these contractions are encoded in the ring formed by all sections of all line bundles on $X$, the Cox ring of $X$. In this talk, we discuss the birational geometry and the Cox ring of blowups of projective spaces at points in general position. For that, we explore Gale duality, a correspondence between sets of $n=r+s+2$ points in projective spaces $\mathbb{P}^s$ and $\mathbb{P}^r$.
tags: Conference on 100 Years of Noetherian Rings

proof_stages.label: Blowups and surface MMP motivation
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up blowups of projective space at general points and recalls that, for surfaces, birational simplification proceeds by finding and contracting (-1)-curves.

proof_stages.label: Cremona-Weyl action for surface blowups
proof_stages.summary: Standard Cremona transformations act as reflections on the Neron-Severi space, preserve nef/effective cones and (-1)-curves, and yield the finite-versus-infinite dichotomy for plane blowups according to the associated Coxeter/Weyl group.

proof_stages.label: Cox rings and Mori dream spaces
proof_stages.summary: The classical finiteness condition is recast as finite generation of the Cox ring, defining Mori dream spaces and explaining why finite generation packages the variety's contractions.

proof_stages.label: Mori chambers and small modifications
proof_stages.summary: The nef, movable, and effective cones are organized into a Mori chamber decomposition, with the movable cone decomposing into nef cones of small modifications.

proof_stages.label: Classification of Mori-dream point blowups
proof_stages.summary: For blowups of P^n at m general points, Mukai's Cremona/Coble-pairing argument gives non-finite generation outside the listed range, while Castravet-Tevelev prove finite generation inside it, and the range is related to log Fano geometry.

proof_stages.label: Gale duality compatible with Cremona action
proof_stages.summary: Gale duality is introduced as a linear-algebraic correspondence between m points in P^r and P^s, and its compatibility with Cremona transformations and Coble-pairing isometries is established as the bridge between dual blowups.

proof_stages.label: n+3-point moduli realization by parabolic bundles
proof_stages.summary: In the extremal Mori-dream case of n+3 points, the Gale-dual configuration on P^1 gives moduli of parabolic rank-two bundles whose Bauer weight chambers realize the Mori chamber decomposition of the blowup.

proof_stages.label: n+4-point moduli realization and partial chamber decomposition
proof_stages.summary: For n+4 points, the Gale-dual blowup surface supports moduli of rank-two sheaves with fixed Chern classes, yielding the original blowup for a suitable polarization and a chamber decomposition on the K-negative side even beyond the Mori-dream range.
